skill.name: maxhub-instagram
skill.description: Instagram 全场景数据查询助手。支持V1/V2/V3三个版本API，覆盖用户信息、帖子、Reels、Stories、评论、搜索、话题、地点等全功能。
skill.summary: Instagram data query assistant with V1/V2/V3 API support for user profiles, posts, Reels, Stories, comments, search, hashtags, and locations.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: All 1066 static findings are false positives. This skill is documentation-only (markdown files) providing API reference for an Instagram data service. The 'external_commands' findings are curl examples in code blocks. The 'env_access' findings document the required MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable properly. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 'obfuscation' findings stem from Chinese text in bilingual documentation. No malicious intent, executable code, or credential exfiltration detected.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: URLs like https://www.aconfig.cn and https://www.instagram.com appear in markdown documentation as API base URLs and example links. These are legitimate documentation entries, not hardcoded endpoint URLs in executable code.

low_findings.title: Shell commands in code blocks (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: Curl commands shown in markdown code blocks are API usage examples for users, not executable script files. The backtick detection is a pattern match on markdown code fence content, not actual shell execution.

low_findings.title: API key environment variable documentation (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: MAXHUB_API_KEY is documented as a required environment variable with proper sensitivity marking. The skill explicitly instructs to keep API key values out of output. This is proper credential management documentation.

low_findings.title: Cryptographic algorithm mentions (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged API parameter descriptions in documentation. No cryptographic algorithms are implemented. Text descriptions contain only natural language API documentation.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance terms in API doc (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: Terms like 'fetch_user_info', 'fetch_user_followers', 'search_users' are legitimate API endpoint names for an Instagram data query skill. These describe data retrieval functions, not reconnaissance activity.

low_findings.title: High entropy text detection (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: Chinese characters in bilingual documentation triggered the entropy heuristic. SKILL.md contains Chinese text alongside English translations, which naturally produces high entropy scores unrelated to obfuscation.

low_findings.title: Dangerous combination heuristic (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: The 'code execution + network + credential access' combination is triggered by markdown documentation containing curl examples, API URLs, and environment variable references together. No actual code exists - all are documentation patterns.

content.user_title: Query Instagram user data, posts, and hashtags with the MaxHub API
content.value_statement: Marketing teams and content creators need Instagram data but lack easy API access. This skill lets you search users, analyze posts and comments, explore hashtags and locations through the MaxHub API using natural language queries.
actual_capabilities: Search Instagram users by keyword and retrieve detailed profile information including follower counts and verification status
Fetch post details, comments, likes, and oEmbed data by URL or shortcode with pagination support
Search hashtags, locations, and music to discover trending content and explore posts by topic or geography
Retrieve user posts, Reels, Stories, and Highlights with full media metadata and pagination
Translate between Media IDs and shortcodes for post URL construction
Support bilingual queries in English and Chinese with localized number formatting
limitations: Requires a valid MAXHUB_API_KEY from aconfig.cn to make API calls
Data accuracy depends on the third-party API provider, not official Instagram APIs
Stories data is ephemeral and only available within 24 hours of posting
Some API endpoints may have rate limits or return incomplete data for private accounts

use_cases.title: Brand social media monitoring
use_cases.description: Track brand mentions and user-generated content on Instagram by searching relevant hashtags, analyzing post engagement, and monitoring comment sentiment over time.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professionals monitoring brand presence on Instagram

use_cases.title: Influencer marketing analysis
use_cases.description: Evaluate potential influencer partners by searching for creators, analyzing their follower demographics, post engagement rates, and content performance metrics.
use_cases.target_user: PR and partnership teams evaluating influencer collaboration opportunities

use_cases.title: Content trend research
use_cases.description: Discover trending visual content and creative patterns by exploring hashtags, locations, and music to find high-engagement posts for content inspiration.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers seeking data-driven inspiration

prompt_templates.title: Find Instagram user profile
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for the Instagram user with username [username] and give me their profile details including follower count, post count, bio, and whether they are verified.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick lookup of a specific Instagram profile

prompt_templates.title: Analyze post engagement
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the post at URL [post_url] and analyze its engagement. Show me the total likes, comment count, top comments, and give a brief engagement analysis.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate performance of a specific Instagram post

prompt_templates.title: Search hashtag content
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for the hashtag [hashtag_name] on Instagram and show me the top posts using this tag. Include the post author, like count, and comment count for each result.
prompt_templates.scenario: Discover popular content under a specific topic

prompt_templates.title: Deep user analysis report
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full analysis of Instagram user [username]. Search for their profile, get their recent posts and Reels, analyze engagement rates, identify top-performing content, and provide a summary of their audience and content strategy.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive evaluation of an Instagram account for partnership decisions

best_practices: Use the search_users endpoint first to find the correct user ID before making detailed profile queries to avoid wasted API calls
Enable pagination for large result sets by passing pagination_token from previous responses to retrieve complete data
Match output language to the user query language for consistent bilingual support
anti_patterns: Do not expose the MAXHUB_API_KEY value in any output or error messages to prevent credential leakage
Do not request all data types at once - make targeted queries for specific user, post, or hashtag data to stay within rate limits
Do not assume all API versions return identical data structures - check the versioned documentation before parsing responses

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a MAXHUB_API_KEY from aconfig.cn. Register at the website, create an API key in your dashboard, and set it as an environment variable.

faq.question: What versions of the Instagram API does this support?
faq.answer: This skill supports V1, V2, and V3 of the MaxHub Instagram API. Each endpoint specifies which version to use. V3 generally has the most complete data.

faq.question: Can I search for private Instagram profiles?
faq.answer: The API can find private accounts and return basic profile information. However, private account posts, followers, and following data will not be accessible.

faq.question: What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports both English and Chinese. It detects the user language and responds with matching output, including localized number formatting.

faq.question: How do I handle pagination for large data sets?
faq.answer: Most endpoints return a pagination_token or cursor in the response. Pass this value to the next request to get more results. Continue until no more pages are available.

faq.question: Is this an official Instagram API?
faq.answer: No, this uses the MaxHub third-party API which aggregates Instagram data. Data obtained is for reference only and accuracy depends on the provider.
